refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness, and environmental factors. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle (load mass, terrain, distance, ambient conditions). Verify that measured p95 ≤ CT_target. Cross-check telemetry logs for safety event correlation (no safety alerts during cycle time measurement runs).
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control responsiveness (latency, jitter) sufficient to meet cycle time target without safety compromise.
FR-2.3: The Safety Services shall enforce cycle time constraints that do not conflict with safety-critical interlocks or emergency stop procedures.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start, end, sub-phase timestamps) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2a: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation timing and sequencing to minimize load→travel→dump cycle duration while maintaining safety margins.
DP-2b: Implement low-latency teleoperation control path (network prioritization, predictive control) to reduce operator-induced cycle time variance.
DP-2c: Design Safety Services to enforce cycle time guardrails (e.g., max speed limits, mandatory pause points) that are compatible with KPI target.
DP-2d: Establish telemetry pipeline (sensor→edge→cloud) with sub-100ms latency for cycle time event logging and real-time KPI tracking.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2: Decompose cycle time into vehicle actuation performance (DP-2a), teleoperation control latency (DP-2b), safety-compatible guardrails (DP-2c), and telemetry instrumentation (DP-2d). Integrate these DPs via a cycle time orchestration layer that coordinates vehicle commands, safety checks, and telemetry capture to achieve p95 ≤ CT_target.

potential_sources: DP-2a (vehicle actuation) and DP-2c (safety guardrails) are coupled: aggressive cycle time optimization may conflict with safety-imposed speed/acceleration limits, requiring trade-off analysis.
DP-2b (teleoperation latency) and DP-2c (safety interlocks) are coupled: low-latency control must not bypass safety checks; safety response time must not degrade cycle time unacceptably.
DP-2d (telemetry) depends on DP-2a, DP-2b, DP-2c outputs; telemetry latency itself can affect real-time KPI visibility.
coupling_check.notes: The coupling is expected and manageable at the next decomposition level. However, the current DP formulation is too coarse to resolve the trade-offs. Recommend zigzag down to sub-FR level to establish explicit coupling constraints and interface contracts.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle actuation physics and network latency constraints. Risk: If CT_target is unrealistic, no DP can satisfy it; requires validation against vehicle performance envelope.

Assumption: Safety guardrails can be tuned to allow cycle time optimization without compromising safety margins. Risk: Safety-critical interlocks may impose hard constraints that make CT_target infeasible; requires safety analysis.
Assumption: Telemetry latency (sub-100ms) is achievable with available network infrastructure. Risk: Site network conditions may not support required latency; requires network characterization.

Assumption: Operator skill and fatigue do not significantly degrade cycle time performance. Risk: Tele-operator workload and situational awareness may introduce variance; requires human factors analysis.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2 by explicitly stating the cycle time budget allocation across vehicle actuation (e.g., 60% of target), teleoperation control (e.g., 20%), and safety overhead (e.g., 20%). This will clarify the coupling constraints and enable sub-level decomposition with measurable targets for each DP.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. At this level, the IM assignment reflects that cycle time KPI is a system-level outcome that requires orchestration across all three sub-modules and their interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exports cycle time events (load start, travel start, dump start, cycle end) and performance metrics (p95, p99, mean cycle time) to external monitoring/analytics systems. Telemetry includes timestamps, cycle phase, and safety event correlation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands (load location, dump location, cycle parameters) from Site Manager or autonomous planning system. Tasking includes duty cycle specification (load mass, terrain type, distance) that defines the context for cycle time measurement.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits SafetyAlert signals when cycle time optimization conflicts with safety constraints or when safety interlocks impose delays. Alerts inform Safety Manager and Tele-operator of safety-driven cycle time impacts.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution depends on Network Link for teleoperation control commands (Tele-operator → Vehicle) and telemetry feedback (Vehicle → monitoring systems). Network latency directly impacts cycle time performance and must be characterized.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for cycle time KPI orchestration. All sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution scope. Minimal diffusion: cycle time logic is localized to NGHE Solution integration layer.

Vehicle module owns actuation performance (DP-2a) and contributes significantly to cycle time. However, cycle time is not solely a Vehicle responsibility; it depends on teleoperation control and safety constraints. Assigning cycle time KPI solely to Vehicle would create diffusion across Teleoperation and Safety Services.

Teleoperation module owns control latency (DP-2b) and operator interface, which affect cycle time variance. However, Teleoperation does not own vehicle actuation or safety guardrails. Assigning cycle time KPI to Teleoperation would diffuse responsibility across Vehicle and Safety Services.

Safety Services owns safety-compatible guardrails (DP-2c) that constrain cycle time optimization. However, Safety Services does not own vehicle actuation or teleoperation control. Assigning cycle time KPI to Safety Services would create high diffusion and conflict with safety-first design principles.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI is a system-level performance outcome that emerges from the integrated behavior of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate IM boundary because it owns the orchestration logic, telemetry pipeline, and integration interfaces that enable cycle time measurement and optimization across all sub-modules. Ownership at NGHE Solution level ensures that cycle time trade-offs (e.g., safety vs. speed) are made at the system level with full visibility to all stakeholders.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution IM boundary may be too coarse to assign clear ownership for cycle time sub-components (vehicle actuation, teleoperation latency, safety overhead). Recommend decomposing NGHE Solution into sub-modules with explicit cycle time budgets.

Risk: Telemetry interface (out) depends on Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to provide cycle time event data. If sub-modules do not instrument their cycle time contributions, NGHE Solution cannot fulfill telemetry obligation. Recommend explicit telemetry contracts in sub-module interfaces.

Risk: SafetyAlert interface (out) requires NGHE Solution to detect and report safety-driven cycle time impacts. This requires real-time monitoring of safety interlocks and their latency effects. Recommend explicit safety monitoring instrumentation in Safety Services module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine NGHE Solution IM boundary by defining explicit sub-module interfaces for cycle time orchestration: (1) Vehicle → NGHE Solution: cycle phase events and actuation timing; (2) Teleoperation → NGHE Solution: control latency and operator commands; (3) Safety Services → NGHE Solution: safety constraint status and guardrail latency. These interfaces will enable NGHE Solution to aggregate cycle time data and enforce orchestration logic without diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR is clear and measurable (p95 ≤ CT_target), but the selected DP is too coarse and exhibits coupling between vehicle actuation, teleoperation latency, and safety guardrails. The DP does not explicitly resolve the trade-offs or allocate cycle time budgets across sub-components. Recommend revising DP to include explicit budget allocation and coupling constraints before proceeding to IM assignment.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Cycle time orchestration logic may be distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules if sub-module interfaces are not explicitly defined. Recommend centralizing orchestration logic in NGHE Solution integration layer.

Diffusion Risk 2: Telemetry instrumentation for cycle time events may be duplicated across sub-modules if telemetry contracts are not established. Recommend defining a single telemetry pipeline owned by NGHE Solution.

Diffusion Risk 3: Safety-driven cycle time constraints may be enforced in multiple locations (Safety Services, Vehicle, Teleoperation) if ownership is not clear. Recommend centralizing safety constraint enforcement in Safety Services with explicit interface to NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM boundary is appropriate for cycle time KPI ownership at the system level. However, the boundary must be refined to include explicit sub-module interfaces for cycle time orchestration, telemetry, and safety constraint propagation. The current IM assignment is high-level and requires decomposition to sub-module level to establish clear ownership and interface contracts.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-2 (cycle time KPI) is feasible via telemetry-based measurement of p95 cycle time across representative duty cycles. The NGHE Solution IM boundary includes Telemetry interface (out) that enables KPI reporting. Verification requires: (1) instrumentation of cycle phase events in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, (2) aggregation of cycle time data in NGHE Solution, (3) statistical analysis of p95 across ≥100 cycles. Feasibility is high if sub-module telemetry contracts are established.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of cycle time responsibility across NGHE Solution, Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services is clear at the system level but requires refinement at the sub-module level. The current IM assignment allocates cycle time KPI to NGHE Solution, which is correct, but does not specify how Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services contribute to the KPI. Recommend decomposing NGHE Solution into sub-modules with explicit cycle time budgets and interface contracts.

recommended_revisions: Revise DP-2 to include explicit cycle time budget allocation across vehicle actuation (DP-2a), teleoperation control (DP-2b), and safety overhead (DP-2c). Example: DP-2a ≤ 60% of CT_target, DP-2b ≤ 20% of CT_target, DP-2c ≤ 20% of CT_target.

Revise DP-2 to explicitly state coupling constraints and trade-off resolution strategy. Example: 'If safety guardrails impose latency > 20% of CT_target, escalate to Safety Manager for risk acceptance.'

Refine NGHE Solution IM boundary to include explicit sub-module interfaces: (1) Vehicle.CyclePhaseEvents → NGHE Solution.CycleOrchestration, (2) Teleoperation.ControlLatency → NGHE Solution.CycleOrchestration, (3) SafetyServices.ConstraintStatus → NGHE Solution.CycleOrchestration.

Establish telemetry contract: NGHE Solution shall collect cycle phase events from Vehicle and Teleoperation, aggregate into cycle time metrics, and export via Telemetry interface with sub-100ms latency.
Establish safety contract: Safety Services shall enforce cycle time guardrails and report safety-driven delays via SafetyAlert interface to NGHE Solution for KPI impact analysis.

decision.justification: The refined FR-2 is clear and measurable, but the selected DP exhibits coupling between vehicle actuation, teleoperation latency, and safety guardrails that cannot be resolved at the current level of abstraction. The IM assignment to NGHE Solution is correct at the system level, but requires decomposition to sub-module level to establish explicit ownership, interface contracts, and cycle time budgets. Recommend zigzag down to decompose FR-2 into sub-FRs (FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-2.4) and refine DPs with explicit budget allocation and coupling constraints before proceeding to sub-module IM assignment.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope (target: ≤60% of CT_target).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control responsiveness (latency, jitter) sufficient to meet cycle time target without safety compromise (target: ≤20% of CT_target).
FR-2.3: The Safety Services shall enforce cycle time constraints that do not conflict with safety-critical interlocks or emergency stop procedures (target: ≤20% of CT_target overhead).
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start, end, sub-phase timestamps) for KPI verification with sub-100ms latency.

revise.why: The selected DP-2 is too coarse and does not explicitly allocate cycle time budgets across sub-components or resolve coupling constraints. Recommend revising DP-2 to include: (1) explicit budget allocation (e.g., vehicle actuation ≤60%, teleoperation ≤20%, safety overhead ≤20%), (2) coupling constraints and trade-off resolution strategy, (3) explicit sub-module interface contracts for cycle time orchestration, telemetry, and safety constraint propagation.

assumptions: Assumption-1: CT_target is achievable within vehicle actuation physics and network latency constraints. Validation required: vehicle performance envelope analysis and network characterization.
Assumption-2: Safety guardrails can be tuned to allow cycle time optimization without compromising safety margins. Validation required: safety analysis and trade-off study.
Assumption-3: Telemetry latency (sub-100ms) is achievable with available network infrastructure. Validation required: network latency measurement and characterization.
Assumption-4: Operator skill and fatigue do not significantly degrade cycle time performance. Validation required: human factors analysis and operator training assessment.
Assumption-5: Cycle time KPI is independent of load mass, terrain type, and ambient conditions within the agreed duty cycle. Validation required: sensitivity analysis across duty cycle parameters.
open_risks: Risk-1: CT_target may be unrealistic given vehicle actuation physics and network latency constraints. Mitigation: Perform vehicle performance envelope analysis and network characterization early in design phase.

Risk-2: Safety-critical interlocks may impose hard constraints that make CT_target infeasible. Mitigation: Conduct safety analysis and establish safety-performance trade-off acceptance criteria with Safety Manager.

Risk-3: Teleoperation control latency may exceed 20% of CT_target due to network conditions or operator workload. Mitigation: Implement predictive control and operator assistance features to reduce latency sensitivity.
Risk-4: Cycle time variance (p95 vs. mean) may be high due to operator skill variation or environmental factors. Mitigation: Implement cycle time monitoring and operator feedback to reduce variance.

Risk-5: Telemetry instrumentation may introduce latency or overhead that degrades cycle time performance. Mitigation: Design telemetry pipeline with minimal latency impact (e.g., asynchronous logging).
evidence_needed: Evidence-1: Vehicle performance envelope (actuation timing, speed, acceleration limits) under agreed duty cycle conditions.
Evidence-2: Network latency characterization (mean, p95, p99) under site conditions.
Evidence-3: Safety analysis report documenting safety-critical interlocks and their latency impact on cycle time.
Evidence-4: Teleoperation control latency measurement (operator command → vehicle response) under representative scenarios.
Evidence-5: Cycle time measurement data from ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle.
Evidence-6: Telemetry pipeline latency measurement and overhead analysis.
Evidence-7: Operator workload and situational awareness assessment for tele-operation scenarios.
